plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-385 RP12.P07.M06.S03-RP12.P08.M02.S14 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-385 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP12.P07 phase (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, M08 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09 Claude Review Packet, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP12.P08 foundation (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition head) as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-386 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-385 is a descriptor-only RP12 billing-core pack (150 units, risk C, range RP12.P07.M06.S03-RP12.P08.M02.S14) that closes the RP12.P07 failure/test/evidence/review/closeout micros and opens the RP12.P08 foundation (Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition head). The pack-level claim is honest and supported: command-evidence and manifest both declare production_ready=false with status "implemented_pending_review"/"implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout"; no enterprise-trust or production claim is overstated. I independently reconciled all seven deliverable categories against the baseline included_units to exactly the claimed distribution (failure_recovery 58, security_audit 10, implementation 21, hermes_evidence 38, fixture 5, test 11, claude_review 7 = 150), and these match the registry CP385_REQUIREMENTS, contract projection, plan pack, and manifest delivery_counts. All 7 required public exports exist (createBillingCoreCp385P07CloseoutP08Foundation{Descriptor,CaseSet}, validateBillingCoreCp385Coverage, validateBillingCoreCp385P07CloseoutP08FoundationDescriptor, createBillingCoreCp385{HermesEvidencePacket,ClaudeReviewPacket,CloseoutHandoff}) and are re-exported through index.js wildcards; model.test.js covers coverage, descriptor-only rows, evidence packets, handoff, and authority boundary; the validate-rp12 script deep-equals the contract to code-generated descriptor/requirements/no_write_attestation; all claimed npm validation scripts exist in package.json. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are extensively attested in freezeCp385Result and the manifest implementation_summary; Claude remains a read-only reviewer and is not promoted to final approval (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false and no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, enforced by validators). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt identity (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, no product runtime). Review-hardening preflight passed (auth exit 0, 1248-byte valid JSON, invalid_attempts_preserved empty), so there is no auth failure, empty stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped evidence to reject. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent because they are produced from this review at the pre-review gate. No P0-P3 defects found; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout, subject to the required human final approval that remains outside this read-only review.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-385 billing core p07 closeout p08 foundation descriptor pack
